findings.title: Access recorder facade repeats the same guard-and-store pattern across every setter
findings.impact: The new facade adds dozens of near-identical setters that each manually check ShouldRecordInternal(), mutate one state field, and rely on Record() to remember a matching Fill step. Future access-log fields now require coordinated edits in the state struct, header declaration, cpp setter, and Record() assembly path, so it is easy for a later change to miss the sampled-out guard or silently omit a field from the emitted request parameters.
findings.fix: Centralize the repeated setter pattern behind small private helpers, for example a guarded SetIfRecording helper for FieldEntry/ObjectKeyState assignments and a single assembly helper per recorder type, so adding a field has one obvious place for the guard and one obvious place for serialization.

findings.title: objectKeysToString overloads duplicate the same truncation and formatting logic
findings.impact: The new RepeatedPtrField and C-array overloads reimplement the same bracket/comma/truncation/total handling already present for vector keys. Any future change to access-log key formatting or truncation limits will need to be made in several slightly different loops, which invites inconsistent output across C++, protobuf, and C API call sites.
findings.fix: Factor the shared formatter into a small helper that accepts key count and a key accessor/span-like callback, then keep each overload as only an adapter from its container representation to that helper.

findings.title: AccessRecorder public header now depends directly on LogSampler
findings.impact: Including log_sampler.h from access_recorder.h couples every AccessRecorder consumer to the sampler implementation even though the public declarations in this header do not expose LogSampler types. That increases rebuild surface and makes the logging subsystem dependency direction harder to reason about as both headers now know about each other’s concepts.
findings.fix: Move the log_sampler.h include to access_recorder.cpp and keep access_recorder.h limited to the AccessRecorder public API and minimal forward declarations.
